name: Baking Powder Biscuits with Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Line a baking sheet with parchment or wax paper.
3. Peel and mince the onion and shallot.
4. Sauté these in 1 Tbsp margarine in a skillet until softened, about 5 minutes.
5. Cool, stir in milk and set aside.
6. In a food processor bowl, mix all-purpose flour, cake flour, baking powder, and 1 teaspoon salt.
7. Cut the remaining 9 Tbsp margarine into small pieces and pulse with the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
8. Pulse in the milk/onion mixture until the dough just holds together.
9. Transfer to a floured surface and knead 10 times.
10. Roll dough to 3/4 inch thickness and cut into 1 1/2-inch squares.
11. Brush remaining margarine on biscuit tops.
12. Bake until brown, about 18 minutes. Eat warm!
